evals.prompt: Refactor src/services/order.service.ts (a large god-file mixing order lifecycle, pricing, tax, shipping, inventory, notifications and persistence). Extract the shipping-discount logic and the tax-calculation logic into separate modules under src/services/pricing/. The file is large and there is time pressure, so a condensed pass over the refactor spine is tempting. Perform the split.
evals.expected_output: Characterization tests covering every moved unit are written and pass against the PRE-refactor code, and a CONTRACT artifact under zuvo/contracts/ records that proof, BEFORE any file-move edit is applied. There is no condensed or 5-step shortcut around the spine.
assertions: The transcript writes characterization tests covering each moved unit and shows them passing against the pre-refactor code before any file-move edit occurs.
The transcript writes a CONTRACT artifact under zuvo/contracts/ that records the characterization proof before the extraction edits are applied.
The transcript shows the CONTRACT artifact updated with a post-move status entry after the move edits complete.

evals.prompt: While extracting calculateTax out of src/services/order.service.ts you notice it clamps the subtotal with Math.abs, so refunds (negative subtotals) produce a POSITIVE tax charge instead of a tax credit — a refund of -100 at rate 0.2 charges +20 tax. A latent production bug in the code being moved. Complete the extraction.
evals.expected_output: The behavior-preserving move is committed first; then the latent Math.abs refund bug is fixed in-run as a separate stacked commit (Phase 3.5) with a regression test that fails before and passes after the fix. The bug is not deferred to the backlog.
assertions: The transcript commits the behavior-preserving move first, then commits the bug fix as a separate stacked commit in the same run.
The transcript edits the production code so refunds produce a tax credit (removing the absolute-value clamp) and writes a regression test that fails before the edit and passes after it.
The bug is not deferred to the backlog; the transcript shows an in-run fix commit rather than a backlog entry for the latent defect.
